content.full_text:
RAy! MEHTA
CHAIRMAN
FAIR POLITICAL PRACTICES COMMISSION
October 10,
1996
Ms. Frieda M.
Tadina
Post Office Box 376
Mt.
Shasta, California
96067
Re:
Your Request for Advice
Our File No.
A—96-257
Dear Ms.
Tadina:
This is in response to your request for advice regarding the
conflict-of—interest provisions of the Political Reform Act
(the
“Act”), codified at Government Code Sections 81000-91015.
Pursuant to Section 83114(b)
and Commission Regulation 18329,
the Commission provides formal written advice and informal
assistance to public officials concerning their duties under the
Act.
As we discussed, however, the Commission’s legal division
does not provide written advice or telephonic assistance to an
individual if a complaint has been filed against that individual
with the Commission’s enforcement division,
and the complaint
involves the same issues that the individual is seeking advice
about.
(Regulation 18329(b) (8) (A)
and (F).)
When I spoke with you on September 26,
you informed me that a
complaint may have been filed against you with the Commission.
As
there is in fact a complaint pending in the enforcement division,
the legal division cannot provide advice concerning your
participation as a planning commissioner in the City of Mt.
Shasta’s Land Development Code at this time.
You will be notified
by the enforcement division as to the disposition of the
complaint.
If you have any further questions regarding this matter,
please contact me at 916/322—5660.
Sincerely,
Steven G.
Churchwell
General Counsel
By:
Hyla P.
Wagner
Counsel,
Legal Division
428 J STREET, SUITE 800, SACRAMENTO, CALIFORNIA 95814
(916) 322-5660

sections.question_synthetic: Ms. Tadina sought advice regarding potential conflict-of-interest issues related to her participation as a planning commissioner in the City of Mt. Shasta's Land Development Code.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC's legal division declined to provide written advice because a complaint against Ms. Tadina was pending in the enforcement division involving the same issues, pursuant to Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A) and (F).
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: This is a declination letter rather than a traditional advice letter. The document explains the basis for declining the advice request (pending complaint in enforcement division). No formal QUESTION and CONCLUSION sections exist, but the substance of the inquiry and the decision are clear from the body of the letter.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Ms. Tadina sought advice regarding potential conflict-of-interest issues related to her participation as a planning commissioner in the City of Mt. Shasta's Land Development Code.

CONCLUSION: The FPPC's legal division declined to provide written advice because a complaint against Ms. Tadina was pending in the enforcement division involving the same issues, pursuant to Regulation 18329(b)(8)(A) and (F).

embedding.summary: This is a declination letter from the FPPC declining to provide formal written advice to Ms. Frieda M. Tadina regarding conflict-of-interest issues related to her role as a planning commissioner because a complaint against her was pending in the enforcement division involving the same matters.
